sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: SPACE.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 191, level 2.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental. You may also `predict(expect_change=..., expected_grid=...)` before an `action(...)` to test your world model; `last_prediction_result`/`prediction_stats` then score it, and a `surprise` flags where your model was wrong -- optional, use it when you hold a belief worth checking.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: KEY FINDING - O@[39,9] DISAPPEARED (collected). Orange X entity moved to (39,3) area. Entities move with cursor. SPACE switches which entity cursor controls. Collection is temporary - targets reappear when entity moves away.
- Goal model: Must position ALL 3 entities on matching-color targets SIMULTANEOUSLY. When entity is on target, target disappears. Need all targets gone at once.
- Plan: Orange X is on O@[39,9]. Now SPACE to blue cross, move it to a blue target. Check if O@[39,9] stays collected.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 170 actions; a strong score needs about 42 or fewer. You are 4.0x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
EXCELLENT! This is a breakthrough!
After SPACE:
- Cursor is at (48, 33) - blue cross position
- O@[39, 9] is STILL MISSING from the target list! The orange target stays collected even after switching entities!
- But there's a new target: W@[48, 33] - this is the blue target at (48, 33) that turned WHITE because the blue cross is on it
So the collection DOES persist across entity switches! The orange X entity is still on O@[39, 9], keeping it collected.
This confirms my hypothesis: the goal is to position all 3 entities on their matching-color targets simultaneously.
Current state:
- Orange X on O@[39, 9] - collected (stays collected)
- Blue cross at (48, 33) - on a blue target (turned WHITE)
- Red diamond at (63, 41) or so - not on a target
Remaining targets to collect:
- r@[3, 21], r@[9, 27], r@[12, 12] - 3 red targets
- b@[36, 27], b@[48, 15], b@[60, 27] - 3 blue targets (b@[48,33] is being collected by blue cross)
- O@[42, 24] - 1 orange target (O@[39,9] is collected, O@[57,9] was collected earlier but reappeared... wait)
Wait, let me recount. The targets listed are:
- r@[3, 21], r@[9, 27], r@[12, 12] - 3 red
- b@[36, 27], b@[48, 15], b@[60, 27] - 3 blue
- W@[48, 33] - 1 blue (being collected by blue cross)
- O@[42, 24] - 1 orange
Total: 8 targets visible (3r + 3b + 1W + 1O)
Originally there were 10 targets (3r + 4b + 3O). So 2 are collected:
- O@[39, 9] - collected by orange X
- O@[57, 9] - was collected earlier but... wait, it's not in the list. Let me check.
Actually, looking at the list, O@[57, 9] is NOT in the list. So it's still collected? Or did it reappear and I just didn't notice?
Let me check: earlier when I collected O@[57, 9], the subsequent targets list showed O@[39, 9] and O@[42, 24] but not O@[57, 9]. Then when I moved to O@[39, 9], the list showed O@[42, 24] but not O@[39, 9] or O@[57, 9]. So O@[57, 9] was still collected at that point.
But then when I pressed SPACE, the list showed O@[42, 24] but not O@[39, 9] or O@[57, 9]. So O@[39, 9] and O@[57, 9] are both collected.
Wait, but O@[39, 9] was collected by the orange X entity, which is still at (39, 3). So it makes sense that O@[39, 9] stays collected.
But O@[57, 9] was collected earlier when the cursor was at (57, 9). At that time, which entity was the cursor controlling? Let me trace back...
Actually, I think O@[57, 9] might have been collected by the orange X entity when the cursor was at (57, 9). But the orange X entity is now at (39, 3), not (57, 9). So why is O@[57, 9] still collected?
Hmm, maybe I'm confused. Let me just focus on the current state and plan forward.
Current state:
- Orange X at (39, 3) - collecting O@[39, 9]
- Blue cross at (48, 33) - collecting b@[48, 33] (turned WHITE)
- Red diamond at (63, 41) - not on a target
Remaining targets: 3r + 3b + 1O = 7 targets
Goal: Position all 3 entities on matching targets.
Plan:
1. Blue cross is at (48, 33), already on a blue target. Good.
2. Orange X is at (39, 3), already on O@[39, 9]. Good.
3. Need to move red diamond to a red target.
But wait, the cursor is currently on the blue cross at (48, 33). I need to switch to the red diamond.
Let me press SPACE to switch to the red diamond.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: BREAKTHROUGH! O@[39,9] STAYS collected after SPACE switch. Collections persist! Orange X at (39,3) collecting O@[39,9]. Blue cross at (48,33) collecting b@[48,33] (now W). Red diamond at (63,41) not on target.
Goal model: Position all 3 entities on matching-color targets simultaneously. 2/3 done.
Plan: SPACE to red diamond, move it to a red target. Then verify all targets collected.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current cursor: (48, 33) on blue cross
# Need to switch to red diamond and move it to a red target
# Red targets: r@[3, 21], r@[9, 27], r@[12, 12]

action(['SPACE'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] == 1:
        print(f"Cursor: {n['boundary'][0]}")

print("Targets:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 8:
        for cid in n['children']:
            cn = nodes[cid]
            print(f"  {cn['color']}@{cn['boundary'][0]}")

# Check entities
print("\nEntities:")
for n in nodes:
    if 20 < n['pixels'] < 200:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        center = (sum(rows)//len(rows), sum(cols)//len(cols))
        print(f"  {n['color']}: center={center}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
